contextual.title: An Assessment of Rapid Streptococcal Tests in Community Clinics in Israel
contextual.text:
Summary: There is a large over-use of antibiotics in family medicine, especially in upper respiratory tract infections.~This study is designed to determine if the use of rapid Streptococcal tests in primary care clinics can lower the rate of antibiotic use while not missing bacterial infections.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 sore throat 

 at least two Centor criteria: 

 fever > 38 deg C or history of fever 

 enlarged cervical lymph nodes 

 tonsillar exudate 

 lack of cough 

 age 3-14 years 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 antibiotic treatment in preceding 7 days 

 no informed consent
